title: Hotel Management Accountant
description: A five\-star career awaits at Carton House! We invite you to begin your next career chapter at Carton House \- a storied and dynamic five\-star resort destination and the first and only member of the Fairmont Hotel collection in Ireland. Nurturing a working environment built on fairness, inclusivity, and growth for all team members, we deliver the highest standard of service, turning moments into memories for all of our guests. We are currently recruiting a **Hotel Management Accountant** to join our Finance Team team at Carton House. **Key Responsibilities:** * Support the Director of Finance in managing the day to day financial operations of the hotel. * Assisting with the preparation of monthly management accounts and P\&L reporting * Completing balance sheet reconciliations and maintaining strong financial controls. * Assisting with revenue reporting and financial analysis across the hotel. * Supporting budgeting and forecasting processes. * Post monthly journals to Sun accounting system. * Reconcile a number of bank accounts. * Preparing financial reports and assisting with audits and compliance requirements. * Working closely with operational managers to ensure financial procedures are followed. * Identifying opportunities to improve reporting, controls and financial processes. * Mentor and train finance team members and graduates. Contribute to the financial performance and smooth running of the finance department. * **Key Requirements:** * The role will suit and experienced hotel financial controller, hotel accountant, finance supervisor or accounts manager within a hotel or hospitality business. * A recognized accounting qualification or being part qualified is desirable. * Previous experience within hotel or hospitality finance preferred. * Strong Analytical ability and excellent attention to detail. * Strong communication and organisational skills. * Confidence working with department heads and operational managers. * A proactive approach and a genuine interest in the hotel finance. * Exceptional analytical abilities. * Knowledge of IT systems(Procure wizard, Opera cloud, Alkimii) * Proven people management and leadership skills. **Why work for Carton House, A Fairmont Managed Hotel?** * To be part of Accor's large global hospitality network. * Employee benefit card offering discounted rates in Accor Hotels worldwide. * Refer a Friend bonus of €250\. * Learning and development opportunities. * Employee Assistance Programme. * Strong team focus and team atmosphere. * Meals and uniform are provided. * Free parking. * Team\-building and social events organised throughout the year in line with our successful Awards and Recognition Scheme. * Training is provided both on\-the\-job and through organised training seminars with both internal and external trainers.

title: Hotel Management Graduate Programme
description: **Ready to kick\-start your career in hospitality?** Our Manager in Training (MIT) Graduate Programme commences in September and is your chance to step into leadership with a structured 18\-month journey designed to build your confidence, skills and future. What you'll gain: * The opportunity to learn directly from experienced leaders across the business * A comprehensive understanding of hotel operations * Exposure to real challenges and responsibilities in a supportive setting * A clear pathway to develop your career with structured guidance and mentorship. * Click on the link below to find out more: https://online.flippingbook.com/view/541185829/ Who we are looking for: * Due to graduate or recently graduated with a relevant degree (Hospitality or Tourism \- however if you do not have a degree in these fields, but have experience in hospitality, we would still love to hear from you) * Excellent communication, teamwork and interpersonal skills * Ability to adapt to a fast paced environment * Passion for hospitality, guest satisfaction * Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends * Open to travel within Ireland for the hotel rotations * Those seeking operational roles within the hotel industry (F\&B, Rooms, Front Office, M\&E and Reservations) If you are motivated, eager to learn and ready to take on new challenges, this programme will provide you with the tools and experience to succeed as a future manager. **Closing date 24th April** Please note we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role, so you must be authorised to work in Ireland **About us:** The MHL Hotel Collection is a collection of unique and individual hotels in key city and resort locations throughout Ireland. From the bustling city centres of Dublin, Galway, Limerick and Belfast , to picturesque and historic estates close to Wicklow and Galway City, each of our hotels has its own distinct personality and charm. *Due to the high volume of applications received, we regret that we are only able to contact candidates whose experience and profile closely match the requirements of the role. We sincerely thank all applicants for their interest and for taking the time to apply*

title: Implementation Consultant (SaaS, €75k)
description: If you thrive on delivering complex, technical solutions, enjoy building things from scratch, and are ready to take ownership of post\-sale success for enterprise clients \- this is your next challenge. You're applying for a fast\-growing, globally trusted software (Series D, $100m ARR) company helping large organisations implement critical, highly customised solutions who are looking for a **Senior Implementation Consultant** to lead end\-to\-end delivery for strategic enterprise clients You'll be working directly with senior stakeholders and cross\-functional teams. It's a **hands\-on, high\-impact post\-sale delivery** , where your technical expertise, problem\-solving, and ability to thrive in ambiguity will drive success. **What You’ll Own:** * Lead **end\-to\-end post\-sale implementations** for enterprise clients. * Manage timelines, risks, and escalations while delivering **highly customised solutions** . * Collaborate closely with Engineering, Product, Account Management, and Customer Success teams. * Translate complex customer requirements into **practical, technical implementations** . * Resolve complex technical and delivery issues, ensuring smooth, timely outcomes. * Contribute to and improve implementation methodologies and best practices. * Mentor and support junior implementation team members. **What You Bring:** * 5\+ years implementing software for **enterprise or strategic customers** . * Proven track record delivering **complex, technical, post\-sale implementations** . * Direct customer\-facing experience with **large global organisations** (e.g., Booking.com, WorldPay). * Strong experience with **APIs (RESTful)** , JSON, JavaScript, SQL, and relational databases. * Comfortable managing multiple implementations in **ambiguous, first\-time delivery scenarios** . * Strong problem\-solving skills and **technical acumen** . * Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Software, or related field preferred; formal PM certification is a plus but not essential. * Experience in SME or scale\-up environments preferred \- **mindset matters more than brand** . **The good stuff:** * **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid – Tues/Wed/Thurs in office) * **Type:** Full\-time, permanent * **Salary:** Up to €75k \& benefits If you've read this far, it means there is clearly a level of interest and you should apply (If you are the right person of course!) If you want to **own enterprise post\-sale delivery** , work with complex systems, and make an impact from day one: APPLY NOW!

title: Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) Consultant
description: Assets and Operations Europe Region DUB0000TF **Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) Consultant** **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin and Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. You see the world through a unique lens and that’s exactly what we need. We embrace how you see things differently – it’s essential for creating a sustainable future. Arup is a global consultancy with advisory and technical expertise across more than 150 disciplines. We guide, plan and design the future of the built environment. Globally, each year we recruit hundreds of graduates to help us see things differently and solve the most complex challenges facing our clients, climate and communities. We have over 600 staff working in Ireland based in Dublin, Cork, Galway, and Limerick. Independently owned and independently minded, Arup is the creative force at the heart of many of the world’s most prominent projects in the built environment. **The Opportunity** We are seeking an ITS consultant to join our Assets Consulting team. In this role, you will contribute to the delivery of IST\-related projects working closely with multidisciplinary teams and clients. **Assets Consulting** Arup’s Assets Consulting team delivers smarter, more sustainable infrastructure solutions across Ireland and beyond. Our team of approximately 35 specialists brings together expertise in civil and electronic engineering, software and web development, technical project management, and transport advisory. We work with clients, primarily in the transport sector, to achieve operational and strategic objectives by managing, operating, maintaining, renewing, and enhancing large infrastructure assets. Our services span strategy development, planning, design, and implementation, as well as supporting maintenance contract management for transportation and digital systems. Our work addresses challenges in Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS), tolling, road user charging, smart cities, traffic signal design, C\-ITS, and Connected and Autonomous Vehicles (CAVs). We also advise on pricing and payments, data\-sharing platforms, future mobility planning, and electromobility. Based within Arup Ireland’s 100\-person Advisory and Digital Services group, we collaborate with clients locally and internationally, combining deep technical expertise with a passion for solving complex challenges. Together, we design and deliver innovative solutions that improve lives and reduce environmental impact. Joining our team means working in a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment where your ideas and curiosity will be valued from day one. As an ITS Consultant, you will contribute to projects that blend engineering, technology, and human\-centered design, helping clients interpret complex data and deliver impactful transport solutions that shape communities and improve lives. **As an Intelligent Transport Systems Consultant at Arup, you will:** * Contribute to Intelligent Mobility projects that deliver high quality pragmatic advice and solutions to Arup’s broad range of Clients and technical challenges. * Utilize technology to provide solutions to transportation consulting projects with a range of clients. * Communicate effectively in written and verbal presentation formats to both internal and external clients. * Developing and enhancing relationships through project delivery. * Work effectively with data and contribute to design of innovative solutions for clients nationally and globally. * Test project objectives, assumptions, and proposed activities against stated goals. * Deliver projects at various stages of development, including design, implementation, testing, commissioning, and operations/maintenance. * Engage stakeholders (including clients) and manage communications. * Undertake focused research and produce summary findings. * Participate in and support the wider development of the team. * Support project leaders on completion of projects’ planning and delivery. * Other Ad\-hoc queries, analysis, training, and project tasks as required. * Supporting the delivery of other projects in the areas of Mobility Pricing and Intelligent Mobility. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for someone who is passionate about Intelligent Mobility. *We also value:* * Minimum of 4\-5 years’ experience in transport projects * Level 8 or above qualifications in a relevant discipline, including but not limited to: + *Civil or Transport Engineering* + *Transport or town planning* + *Intelligent Transport Systems* + *Electrical and Electronic Engineering* + *Telecommunications* + *Computer Engineering or Computer Science* + *Mechatronic Engineering* * Technical experience or demonstrated interest in one of the following areas: Intelligent Transport Systems, electronic tolling, transportation, intelligent mobility, and Connected and Autonomous Vehicles. * Prior experience working in a consultancy environment is desirable. * Experience in one or more of planning, design, procurement or operations. * You have excellent communication, analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Solid planning, analytical and organization skills * Strong numerical and IT skills * Systems integration knowledge and experience a positive. * Ability to work independently as well as in a large multi\-disciplinary team. **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in rewarding our members for their contributions to our shared success. As a trust\-owned company, we have the freedom to pursue meaningful work that aligns with our purpose and values. We also offer a unique profit share program, allowing our members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Benefits that work for you:** * **Professional Growth \& Development**: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, training programs, and mentorship to enhance your skills and advance your career at Arup. * **Global Opportunities**: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * **Financial Well\-being \& Ownership**: Receive a competitive salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing programme. * **Wellbeing and Culture:** At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * **Equality, Diversity and Inclusion:** We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to building a workplace that reflects the rich diversity of the world around us. We actively promote a culture of inclusion where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Investment Banking intern - Dublin
description: We are offering an internship for driven individuals who want serious exposure to the world of investment banking. This is a practical, work\-based opportunity focused on the technical and analytical tasks that underpin live transactions and strategic advisory work. The role gives you direct exposure to how investment decisions are analysed, how companies are valued, and how deal materials are prepared in professional environments. **Responsibilities** * Assist with building financial models and valuation outputs * Conduct market, company, and industry research * Support presentation creation for strategic and transaction\-related projects * Help prepare financial summaries and supporting analysis * Review earnings, reports, filings, and M\&A developments * Contribute to internal and external project work where analytical support is required **Who We Are Looking For** * Students, recent graduates, or candidates with relevant transferable experience * Strong interest in investment banking or corporate finance * Confidence using Excel and PowerPoint * Solid grasp of financial statements and business analysis * Strong work ethic, initiative, and attention to detail **What You Will Get** * Practical experience on analyst\-style tasks * Exposure to valuation, M\&A, and financial decision\-making * Mentorship and commercial insight from experienced professionals * Flexible and remote\-friendly working structure **Compensation** * This role includes a paid component, with further progression based on contribution Y1J5wZo3Jq

title: Investment Operations Specialist - 12 Month Contract
description: Mercer’s Investment Management business, Investment Solutions International (IS), is looking for a motivated and detail\-oriented investment operations specialist to join our Operations team. **This is a 12 month fixed term contract role with potential for permanency subject to performance and business needs** . While prior experience in regulatory reporting or fund expense oversight is a plus, it is not required. We are looking for someone with a basic understanding of fund accounting and a keen interest in learning about European investment products and operational processes. **What can you expect:** Mercer is a fast\-growing investment solutions provider with a broad and expanding client base. Our fund range includes approximately 140 Irish domiciled funds across multiple legal structures, supporting Mercer’s investment and risk management solutions for institutional clients. The Operations team supports a variety of important activities including onboarding new clients, launching funds, managing fund rebalancing, overseeing NAV and valuations, and supporting regulatory and tax compliance. You will work closely with experienced colleagues and third\-party service providers to help ensure smooth and accurate operations. The Regulatory Reporting team focuses on ensuring Mercer meets its regulatory obligations, including reporting under MiFID II, AIFMD, MMFR, EMIR, and PRIIPs KIDs. As an Associate, you will become responsible for reporting processes and gain exposure to regulatory changes and process improvement. In addition, you will support Fund Expenses Oversight liaising closely with our Dublin lead and Mumbai team to ensure all fund expense related tasks are completed accurately and on time. This role offers a great learning environment where you will support \& contribute to key operational and regulatory tasks, develop your knowledge of investment operations, and actively work on team projects. **We will rely on you to:** * Learn and develop a solid understanding of Mercer’s fund structures and investment solutions * Support the team in delivering accurate and timely regulatory reporting and fund expense oversight * Act as primary subject matter expert and support for offshore colleagues. * Assist with data validation, monitoring, and basic analysis to ensure data quality * Help research and summarize regulatory requirements and fund operations under guidance * Support process improvements and automation initiatives to enhance operational efficiency, driving forward solutions and taking ownership of outcomes. * Collaborate with internal teams and external service providers to support daily operations * Contribute to team projects and support the implementation of new regulatory requirements * Maintain a strong focus on risk management and compliance within your work **What you will need to have:** * 2\-5 years of experience in asset management, fund administration, or investment operations, with relevant academic background (Business, Accounting, Finance, Economics). * Good understanding of fund accounting principles and investment products. * Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn. * Excellent analytical skills and comfort working with data (Excel proficiency required; SQL or other data tools a plus). * Interest in regulatory frameworks and compliance processes (prior experience is a bonus but not essential). * Effective communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a team environment. * Self\-motivated, self\-starter with a positive attitude and eagerness to develop professionally. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and work in a fast\-paced environment. * High performance outlook comfortable taking ownership of work and driving solutions. * Good technology skills including Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) and openness to learning new tools. **What will make you stand out:** * Enthusiasm for building a career in investment operations and regulatory reporting * Proactive approach to learning and problem\-solving * Strong organizational skills and ability to deliver work accurately and on time * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Ability to quickly absorb new information and adapt to change * Confident and comfortable in making decisions. **Why join our team:** * To be part of a company with a strong brand, committed to making a difference in people's lives. * We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders. * We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues, clients and communities. * Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well\-being. \#MercerIS Mercer is a business of Marsh (NYSE: MRSH), a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information about Mercer, visit mercer.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X. Marsh is committed to embracing a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We are an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to providing reasonable support to any candidate with a disability/health condition to allow them to fully participate in the recruitment process. We welcome candidates to contact us at reasonableaccommodations@mmc.com to discuss any specific needs. Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office\-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person. **R\_339553**

title: Investor Relations Associate (Real Estate) - Global Hedge Fund - DUBLIN
description: A multi\-billion dollar AUM global hedge fund are looking to hire an Investor Relations Associate, specific to the firm's Real Estate business, to based out of their Dublin office. Directly supporting a team of high\-calibre investor relations professionals and marketers / fundraisers, the hire will have ownership for tasks such as marketing materials, presentations, investor reports for real estate strategies, whilst also supporting the marketing efforts of the firm for asset\-raisers who will also regularly hand the individual wide ranging, interesting projects. For a role at this level, it carries lots of autonomy and visibility in the firm. Progression into a more senior / IR manager role or a marketing position within the firm can be possible in the future. Candidates should have 2\-5yrs experience in a marketing support / investor relations / client services role. A strong academic record is expected. This is a crucial hire – our client is committed to offering a very attractive increase on the hire’s current base salary. As a ‘total comp payer’, an exceptional performer can also expect to receive a large % of this as a bonus.

title: Junior Accountant
description: **Location:** Dublin City Centre **Salary:** €40k basic plus pension, healthcare **Type:** Full\-time, 18 month contract We’ve partnered with a renowned insurance company who are currently seeking a Junior Accountant to join their Dublin City team. This maternity cover contract will the successful candidate involved in AP, AR and reconciliations. **Key Responsibilities** * Accounts payable * Managing bank accounts on a daily basis * Analyse bank account transactions for postings to the GL * Perform reconciliations as part of payment processes and month end * Assist internal and external auditors * Ad hoc duties as required **Key Skills** * 2\+ years experience in similar role, insurance industry experience is highly advantageous * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Highly organised with excellent attention to detail *Due to the high volume of applications received, we regret that we are unable to respond to all applications. Only those suitable for this position will be contacted*

title: Junior Accountant - Europe
description: At Turner \& Townsend we’re passionate about making the difference. That means delivering better outcomes for our clients, helping our people to realize their potential, and doing our part to create a prosperous society. Every day we help our major global clients deliver ambitious and highly technical projects, in over 130 countries worldwide. Our team is dynamic, innovative, and client\-focused, supported by an inclusive and fun company culture. Our clients value our proactive approach, depth of expertise, integrity and the quality we deliver. As a result, our people get to enjoy working on some of the most exciting projects in the world. **Job Description** The Europe Group Junior Accountant will be involved in several finance processes across the region including consolidation of monthly management accounts, reporting, cashflow, budgeting and forecasting processes. They will be a strong team player with a generic understanding of accounting principles and processes and strong MS Excel knowledge. **Responsibilities** * Supporting integration of new entities, preparing templates, process notes, training materials, assisting with required system set up in place e.g. new entity or cost centre, gathering information on user access rights required, updating consolidated template to include the new information. * Gathering and consolidating financial information on sector level and details on property leases from whole Europe, including new acquisitions. * Supporting the preparation of consolidated monthly management accounts with supporting financial analysis and commentary * Supporting the yearly consolidated Europe budget process and monthly consolidated Europe forecasting process * Assisting in month end reporting for assigned company in line with region/group deadlines and assistance with other month and year end reporting. * Monthly reconciliation of balance sheet accounts of assigned entity * Supporting the preparation of the weekly/monthly cashflow forecast to ensure an accurate representation of future cashflows and balances. * Working on ad\-hoc projects including new systems implementations, business acquisitions, cross border projects etc. * Work across Europe Finance team to support whole team as necessary. * SOX control responsibilities may be part of this role, which are to be adhered to where applicable. Key Stakeholders * RFD, RMD, RFC, Country MDs and Cost centre leads * Europe Finance * Group Finance **Qualifications** * Part Qualified Accountant (CIMA/ACCA/ACA) * Strong Excel and MS office applications experience * Confident individual with good interpersonal skills and ability to work with people at all levels * Accuracy and high\-level attention to detail * General understanding of accounting principles and processes **Additional Information** Our people share our Purpose and Values. Turner \& Townsend provides a great place to work, where everyone can make change happen and influence a better world.​ We champion our people to succeed in both work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive, and flexible working environment that respects work\-life balance.​ Turner \& Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.​ If this sounds like a challenge you’d like to hear more about, please submit an application for our recruitment team to review. ​ Please find out more about us at www.turnerandtownsend.com/​ Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner \& Townsend and our exciting future projects: Twitter Instagram LinkedIn It is strictly against Turner \& Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. title: Junior Consultant - Training Provided
description: Are you an ambitious professional looking to make a significant impact in a new and dynamic field? If you have a passion for people and thrive in fast\-paced environments, we have an exciting opportunity for you! * **Unlimited Earning Potential: Fast\-Paced \& Rewarding Environment** * **Clear and Transparent Career Development – Progress to Leadership** * **Personalised Training – Use your Sales skills in a new exciting way** **What can you expect day to day?** As an Associate Recruitment Consultant, you'll have the freedom to do things differently, supported by colleagues with a wealth of experience, empowering you to learn and grow. No day is the same here at Access, and you will gain experience across a varied range of skills including: * Building relationships with clients and candidates * Managing vacancies from start to finish * Screening, interviewing and shortlisting candidates in the healthcare industry Here at Access, you really can influence your own earning potential, by getting out what you put in. Your energy and dedication will be rewarded in a culture built on celebrating success. **What we are looking for** : * 1 – 2 years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) – No previous recruitment experience is needed. * Passion to succeed and be part of a fast\-paced inclusive environment * Relationship\-focused with the confidence to build strong networks * Collaborative in spirit with a growth mindset to develop a long\-lasting career **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission (Average OTE: Year 1 €47k, Year 2 €75k) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: until 12 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making (all leaders grow inside) * Paid sabbatical * Wellness programme (e.g. time management, skills development, health talks, advise and activities, stress management) * Industry\-leading training programme with 200\+ training modules – Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and team socials If you are curious enough to know more, all you need to do is send us a current CV! francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie ***Please note that only EU Citizens \& Stamp 4 applications will be considered for this role.***

title: Junior Office Assistant
description: Thomas Barry \& Company is seeking a motivated and detail\-oriented Junior Office Assistant to join our team and support our firm’s day\-to\-day business. The ideal candidate will be focused, friendly, and hard\-working, along with possessing strong interpersonal skills. The ideal candidate will also be someone who learns quickly, has strong time\-management skills, and is willing to take initiative. A positive and vibrant in\-office atmosphere is vital to us. We like to live by our motto “People Deal With People”. **Key Duties and Responsibilities:** * Taking and returning phone calls; * Scheduling appointments for the Notary Public; * Receiving, scanning, and assigning mail; * Greeting clients; and * Scanning documents and assisting with file opening. **Other Duties May Include:** * Arranging couriers and town agents; * Recording and updating files and records; * Assisting with organising and maintaining office common areas; * Preparing select documentation during appointments; * Delivering documents locally; and * Other miscellaneous and ad hoc administrative and clerical tasks. **Candidate Requirements** * Leaving Certificate; * Fluent in English; * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills; * Good phone etiquette; * Excellent multitasking and organisational skills; * Good work ethic and willingness to act on own initiative and within a team environment; * Strong attention to detail and problem\-solving skills; * Ability to prioritise and balance multiple time\-sensitive tasks; * Computer literacy and good IT skills; and * Reliable means of transportation into office. Experience working in an administration or secretarial role is an asset but is not required. This role is not suitable for candidates seeking a traineeship or FE1 candidates. **Features of the role include:** * Attractive terms and conditions with opportunity and encouragement for advancement; * A happy and caring work environment with a great and supportive team; * Convenient and easily\-accessed central Dublin location; * TaxSaver and Bike to Work Scheme; * Social events with other employees; and * Exposure to a dynamic work environment in a busy law office. **Working Hours** This position is on\-site at our office in Dublin, from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. on Mondays to Fridays. **Apply Now!** Please send your CV and cover letter to Elif Caliskan at reception@thomasbarry.ie. All applications will be dealt with in the strictest confidence. Thomas Barry \& Company (https://www.thomasbarrysolicitors.ie/) is an equal opportunities employer. title: Junior Recruiter
description: **Hybrid working \- 4 days in office, 1 day WFH** **Salary \- €30,000 basic \+ uncapped commission** TEKsystems’® unmatched success in the IT Services and Talent Management marketplace is driven by one thing – our employees. A thriving IT industry has caused us to grow at a rapid pace and we are looking for people to join our team. We offer great opportunities for advancement, personal and professional growth, and unlimited earning potential. TEKsystems® seeks professional individuals with competitive spirit, drive, team mentality, commitment, perseverance, and a desire to build a long\-term career in a rewarding environment. Don’t worry. No IT experience is needed. We’ve got that covered. Our comprehensive training programme allows you to learn terminology, job functions, and practice areas within the information technology industry. **What you will contribute:** * Recruit top IT candidates and serve as the main point of contact throughout the entire recruiting cycle. * Build and maintain relationships with consultants to match them with the BEST opportunities. * Evaluate experience and opportunities of candidates through our screening process. * Understand our clients’ business and IT initiatives, as well as their specific technical and cultural environments to best match the career goals of consultants with their hiring needs. * Negotiate unique compensation packages (wages, benefits, etc.) to attract and hire candidates for our clients’ needs. **What you bring to the table:** * Interest in a performance\-based environment including outbound calling. * Enthusiasm to network and communicate with others. * Grit, tenacity, and determination. **Extra Points if you have:** * Previous sales/recruiting experience (Internships included) * Experience working in a performance\-driven environment **The Opportunity** * Growth potential with various career paths for recruiters and sales professionals * An elite and continuous training programme within the IT industry * The opportunity to train and mentor others * Diverse and inclusive culture with a family\-like environment The Perks * Unlimited earning potential – competitive base salary \+ uncapped commission * Annual all\-inclusive company incentive trips * Charitable and social responsibility opportunities * Health and well\-being support

title: LIMS Project Analyst
description: **LIMS Project Analyst \- Ireland, Dublin \- 18 Month Contract** Our client is one of the world’s largest pharmaceutical organisations. With continued organisational growth including ongoing investment across the portfolio and capital projects programme at their manufacturing facility in Dublin, an excellent opportunity has arisen for a LIMS Project Analyst to join the team. **What You'll Do** * Configuration or modification of LIMS setups, including microbiology or QC Analytical methods, product specifications, schedulers, standards \& reagents, COA's, Label and printer setup, and associated LIMS Master Data. * Creation and verification of LIMS Master Data as required. * Development of Test Scripts/Test Protocols for formal LIMS test execution as required. * Execution of informal and formal verification testing of the configured LIMS solution in collaboration with Computer Systems Validation (CSV) project resources. * Hosting facilitated review workshops with users to walk them through the configured LIMS, making updates prior to design completion and testing. **What you'll need:** * Bachelor's degree in a life science, IT or a related scientific discipline. * Extensive LIMs administration / Master Data Building. * Experience working in finished product pharmaceuticals/biologics, including analytical chemistry, quality assurance, quality control, development, and/or manufacturing areas. * Thorough understanding of quality systems and cGMP's. * Experience in Master Data Building and / or maintenance of LIMS data. **For more information on this position, please apply with a copy of your CV today!**

title: Landscape Operative - Training and Career progresson
description: **Landscape Operative / General Operative – Training \& Career Progression** **Pay:** €14\.65–€16\.50 per hour **Location:** Dublin / Greater Dublin Area **Job Type:** Full\-time, Permanent **Looking for an outdoor job with real career prospects?** O’Brien Landscaping is seeking **Landscape Operatives / General Operatives** to join our growing team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone who enjoys **working outdoors, staying active and learning practical skills**, and who wants to build a **long\-term career** in the landscaping and construction industry. This is an **entry\-level role**, but it offers genuine opportunities to progress within the company. With the right attitude, reliability and work ethic, this role can lead to a long\-term future in the industry. O’Brien Landscaping is one of Ireland’s leading landscape construction and maintenance companies, delivering high\-quality commercial projects across Dublin and beyond. **Build a Career, Not Just a Job** Many of our team members have developed their careers with us into roles such as: * Supervisor * Foreperson * Machine Operator * Tractor Driver * Specialist Installer * Landscape Maintenance Operative * Management roles We regularly share real staff progression stories and project work on our Instagram page, giving a genuine insight into what it’s like to work with us and how people build careers here. https://www.instagram.com/obrien.landscaping **What You’ll Be Doing** You will work as part of a landscape team helping to deliver a wide range of outdoor projects. Typical work includes: * Planting trees, shrubs and ornamental planting * Laying grass and carrying out soft landscaping works * Soil preparation and groundworks * Assisting with hard and soft landscape installation * Working on commercial landscapes, streetscapes, rooftop projects and sports facilities * Supporting experienced team members while learning practical site skills This is varied, hands\-on outdoor work with the opportunity to gain valuable experience across a wide range of projects. **Where You’ll Work** Most of our work is based in the **Dublin area**, with occasional work in other parts of the country. **What We’re Looking For** We are looking for someone who is: * Interested in building a long\-term career * Comfortable working outdoors in a physical role * Reliable, hardworking and punctual * Positive, respectful and willing to learn * Able to work well as part of a team * Ready to take instruction and develop practical site skills You do **not** need years of experience, but you do need the **right attitude and work ethic**. **Essential Requirements** * Safe Pass * Manual Handling Certificate If you do not currently have these, we can advise you on how to obtain them before starting. **Beneficial Skills / Experience** The following would be an advantage, but are not essential: * Full driving licence * Machine tickets or machinery experience * Working at height experience * Soft or hard landscaping experience * Construction or outdoor work experience **Working Hours** * Typical hours: **7:30am – 4:00pm** * Seasonal variability applies * Overtime and weekend work may be available **What We Offer** * Competitive hourly rate * Overtime opportunities * Bonus opportunities * Company pension * Paid annual leave * Full on\-the\-job training * Career development and progression opportunities * Bike to Work Scheme * Company events * Wellness programme **Why Join O’Brien Landscaping?** This role offers more than just a job — it is an opportunity to build practical skills, gain site experience and develop a long\-term future in a respected and growing company. If you are dependable, willing to learn and interested in working outdoors, this could be the start of a strong career. **Apply Now** If you are hardworking, reliable and interested in building a future in landscaping, we would be pleased to hear from you. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €14\.65\-€16\.50 per hour Expected hours: 35 – 45 per week Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Legal Administrator
description: We are representing one of Ireland’s premier commercial law firms in their search for a skilled Legal Administrator to join their dedicated New Homes team. This is an excellent opportunity to join a growing, award\-winning firm that provides "top\-tier" legal services both in Ireland and internationally. **The Role:** Working closely with property specialists, you will support all stages of new\-build transactions in a fast\-paced, client\-focused environment. Your focus will be on ensuring a smooth day\-to\-day workflow through meticulous documentation management and clear communication. **Key Responsibilities:** * **Matter Management:** Setting up new client matters in Keyhouse, ensuring data accuracy and compliance. * **Financial Support:** Assisting with drafting, reviewing, and finalizing invoices for timely issuance. * **Document Control:** Managing team post, scanning, and saving contract/closing documents to iManage with correct naming standards. * **Closing Support:** Compiling organized and complete contract and closing packs to support client completions. * **Liaison:** Effectively communicating with developers and purchasers' solicitors throughout the transaction lifecycle. **The Successful Candidate:** * **Organized \& Efficient:** Able to manage multiple tasks with strong attention to detail and accuracy. * **System Savvy:** Comfortable using legal systems such as **Keyhouse** and **iManage** (training can be provided). * **Team Player:** Communicates clearly and maintains a proactive, professional approach within a busy team. * **Confidentiality:** High level of professionalism regarding sensitive client information. **Why Apply?** This firm offers a collaborative and inclusive culture with a genuine focus on your professional development and career progression. **Comprehensive Benefits Include:** * Access to associate and partner development programs. * Active firm committees (Social, Wellness, Green, Diversity, and Pro Bono). * On\-site gym and subsidised yoga/fitness classes. * A vibrant social calendar with numerous events throughout the year.

title: Legal Quality Analyst (compliance) I Dublin
description: **Description** **Quality Analyst \- Compliance Operations** **Type:** Permanent position **Shifts:** 5 days/week, Monday\-Friday 8am\-5pm (subject to change), 8 working hours with an hour lunch break Are you passionate about upholding the highest standards of quality and compliance? We are seeking a meticulous Quality Analyst to help maintain and enhance the integrity of our client's platform. In this role, you'll ensure our policies are followed across various compliance areas, from intellectual property to data privacy, specializing in one of our four compliance streams. **What We Offer** * Competitive Rewards: Monthly performance bonuses and employee referral program (refer a friend and earn up to €1,500\) * Comprehensive Benefits: Company pension, medical insurance contributions, and wellness programs * Career Development: Stable, permanent role with regular training and opportunities to advance your career on a global or local level within TELUS Digital * Inspiring Workspace: Vibrant office with relaxation zones and game areas (table tennis, foosball table) * Transport Perks: Parking, Bike to Work scheme, and TaxSaver Leap Cards * Work\-Life Balance: 20 vacation days and wellness programs * Community Impact: Be part of meaningful CSR (corporate social responsibility) projects and community initiatives * Global Exposure: 50\+ benefits and services to choose from in an international environment **Overview Of Tasks** As a Quality Analyst, you'll specialize in one of the following compliance streams: * Contractual Operations (COPS): Ensure our client meets licensing and regulatory obligations with a focus on the music industry. Review and act on potential licensing violations by removing or blocking content, and support global regulatory efforts to balance content access with licensing limits. * Scaled Regulatory Operations (SRO): Ensure our client's compliance with local laws and regulations. Design, build, and scale operational solutions to address the ever\-evolving regulatory landscape, mitigate risks associated with non\-compliance, and collaborate with internal stakeholders. * Privacy Operations: Support our client's platforms by ensuring user data privacy requests (data access, deletion, and portability) are correctly implemented, tested, and aligned with global privacy regulations including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, and other regional requirements. * Intellectual Property Operations: Independently investigate and validate cases across copyright, trademark, counterfeit, and brand rights protection workflows. Ensure adherence to global regulations including the Digital Services Act (DSA) and Digital Millennium Copyright Act (DMCA), identify policy gaps, perform root cause analysis, and recommend improvements to policies and processes. **We Are Looking For Someone Who Can** * Demonstrate a keen eye for catching non\-compliance and inconsistencies with strong attention to detail * Make quick, informed decisions based on guidelines and policies * Excel at managing workflows and maintaining a high level of organization * Articulate complex issues clearly and professionally with strong communication skills * Show passion for maintaining high standards of policy and legal adherence * Demonstrate C1 level English proficiency * Bring experience in a Compliance role (AML, KYC, etc.) or Policy Quality Analyst role * Utilize internal tools and external resources for verification of trademark registrations and validation of copyright claims * Provide regular feedback to QA team leads and policy experts * Collaborate with global teams to ensure consistent quality standards **Join our team and apply now!** Apply today and if you're successful with your initial application, our recruitment department will be in touch within 7 days. Are you worried your profile doesn't meet all the listed requirements? Don't hesitate to apply anyway! We wouldn't like to miss the opportunity of meeting a great candidate. **About us:** Since founded in 1998, excellent customer service is our passion \- in 2020 we went one step further and are now part of TELUS Digital. Since then, over 70,000 team members are providing customer service at its best in more than 50 languages for international and well\-known partners. *At TELUS Digital, we enable customer experience innovation through spirited teamwork, agile thinking, and a caring culture that puts customers first. TELUS Digital is the global arm of TELUS Corporation, one of the largest telecommunications service providers in Canada. We deliver contact center and business process outsourcing (BPO) solutions to some of the world's largest corporations in the consumer electronics, finance, telecommunications and utilities sectors. With global call center delivery capabilities, our multi\-shore, multi\-language programs offer safe, secure infrastructure, value\-based pricing, skills\-based resources and exceptional customer service \- all backed by TELUS, our multi\-billion dollar telecommunications parent.* **About Company** TELUS Digital is the customer experience transformation partner to the world's most admired brands. Our diverse team weaves data, technology, and human ingenuity to deliver differentiated customer journeys, drive operational effectiveness, and scale AI solutions with meaningful value and positive impact. We craft real\-world solutions in the moments that matter, from customer acquisition to lifelong loyalty. Enabled by our global reach \- spanning 78,000 experts in 33 countries \- and deep industry expertise, we help over 600 organizations make the customer experience feel effortless. Our solutions span Data \& AI, Digital Experience \& IT, CX Management and Trust \& Safety. At the core of our innovation is Fuel iX™, an enterprise\-grade generative AI platform that helps clients safely access and optimize leading LLMs to scale their own AI from pilot to production. **Equal Opportunity Employer Statement** At TELUS Digital, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. All aspects of employment, including the decision to hire and promote, are based on applicant's qualifications, merits, competence and performance without regard to any characteristic related to diversity.

title: Lending Business Analyst
description: NTT DATA strives to hire exceptional, innovative and passionate individuals who want to grow with us. If you want to be part of an inclusive, adaptable, and forward\-thinking organization, apply now. **Summary of Role:** * Bring a good understanding of the Lending and Credit domain having worked in similar capacity earlier * Interact with business users and technical team for understanding of functional requirements * Document business requirements and process flows using different tools like JIRA, Confluence, Blue Works, Lucid Chart, MS Visio etc * Analyse and design solutions with the support of Solution Architects * Provide feedback on test cases and support to Quality Assurance for testing * Support in client UAT **Responsibilities of the role:** * Engage with business users to define scope, understand business processes, problem statements and pain areas * Identify key stakeholders and construct stakeholder analysis i.e. how they are affected * Support the identification of risks and mitigation approaches * Participate in client requirement workshops and identify gaps together with high level proposals for their resolution (e.g. development workarounds or change in processes). Ensure any solutions proposed are realistic and within the agreed scope of the project. * Analyse client business requirements, liaise with clients / project teams and prepare business process documents like BRDs, User Stories, Discussion Logs, Workflows etc. Be involved actively in the process till the sign\-off is not obtained from the client. * Participate in Solution design with the Design and Development team to ensure requirements are duly addressed. Ensure Requirement Traceability Matrix is updated to facilitate tracking of requirements coverage. * Make appropriate recommendations that will positively impact operational effectiveness, reduce cost and provide a better customer experience * Conduct relevant research to increase understanding in support of developing comprehensive solutions * Participate in final User Acceptance Testing for all major developments in the domain * Assist in the production of various collaterals such as User Guide, SOP Documents, process flows etc **Experience** * A strong background in Lending domain having good understanding of end\-to\-end lending lifecycle in areas such as lead management, loan origination, loan management and servicing, collateral management and delinquency handling * Should have good understanding of gamut of retail products loans such as Personal Loans, Mortgages, Vehicle Loans, Credit Cards etc and corporate products such as Overdrafts, Working Capital, Bill Discounting, Trade Finance etc * Familiarity with leading banking applications like FinnOne Neo, Finacle, Flexcube, TCS BaNCS, T24 is a plus * Design thinking, ability to manage multiple stakeholder expectations, communicate in a clear non\-ambiguous manner, conduct requirements workshops, drive decision making * Experience in documenting business case, business process modelling, requirement elicitation through workshops * Manage projects from inception through design to delivery * An IT background with a solid grounding in technology * Experience of working in both Agile \& waterfall SDLCs * Communication, group dynamics, collaboration and continuous improvement are core – being best practice driven * Pro\-active, quick learner, ability to effectively work in multi\-cultural and multi\-national teams * Positive attitude and ability to engage with different stakeholders managing scope and expectations skilfully **About NTT DATA** NTT DATA is a $30 billion business and technology services leader, serving 75% of the Fortune Global 100\. We are one of the world's leading AI and digital infrastructure providers, with unmatched capabilities in enterprise\-scale AI, cloud, security, connectivity, data centers and application services. our consulting and Industry solutions help organizations and society move confidently and sustainably into the digital future. As a Global Top Employer, we have experts in more than 50 countries. We also offer clients access to a robust ecosystem of innovation centers as well as established and start\-up partners. NTT DATA is a part of NTT Group, which invests over $3 billion each year in R\&D. Whenever possible, we hire locally to NTT DATA offices or client sites. This ensures we can provide timely and effective support tailored to each client’s needs. While many positions offer remote or hybrid work options, these arrangements are subject to change based on client requirements. For employees near an NTT DATA office or client site, in\-office attendance may be required for meetings or events, depending on business needs. title: Life Sciences Associate Consultant
description: Are you curious about science, medicine, and pharmaceuticals? Do you consider yourself to be a fast learner, proactive, and diligent? FENIX continues to experience significant growth and is seeking a Life Sciences Associate Consultant to support growing global consulting engagements. Our Life Sciences Associate Consultants leverage their analytic, interpretive, scientific, and communication skills to formulate well\-thought\-out insights that support team decision\-making and execution. FENIX Associate Consultants help derive customer insights through the conduct, management, and analysis of primary and secondary qualitative and quantitative research. They assist in writing concise, accurate summaries of industry and market events using their knowledge and context of the pharmaceutical industry as they contribute to successful project deliveries. In this role, you would have the opportunity to gain consulting experience from a team of FENIX experts, help guide and influence decision\-making for a global pharma company, and develop thought leadership in exciting therapy areas. **RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Conduct comprehensive secondary market research (e.g., monitoring competitor websites, earnings calls, clinical trials, and patient/HCP websites) * Identify and conduct primary market research (e.g., interviews of HCPs/KOLs, industry sources) and provide clear and accurate notes to the team * Assist junior team members with various market\-related tasks * Develop consistent and impactful primary CI * Cover key international conferences (either virtually or in\-person), including key clinical sessions, industry booths, and collect primary research * Develop a comprehensive congress planner * Interpret key clinical trials * Leverage problem\-solving skills and industry research methodologies to derive insights and inform project team and client decision\-making * Conduct quantitative research data analyses * ‪Leverage strong project management and organizational skills to ensure that all tasks are performed in a timely manner and that all client deliverable deadlines are met * Synthesize and communicate results to project teams and clients, leveraging consulting language * Assist in authoring relevant, clear, and succinct client implications, executive summaries, and elevator pitches * ‪Play an active role in deliverables, including client presentations and reports, as well as leveraging tools to strengthen client relations ‪ **QUALIFICATIONS:** * Excellent oral and written communication skills in English (fluent or Level C2 Certificate) **REQUIRED** * EU citizenship or the ability to legally live/work in Dublin, Ireland without the need for sponsorship **REQUIRED** * ‪Minimum of a master's degree in life sciences (e.g., biochemistry, pharmacology, biology), pharmaceutical business, or related field **REQUIRED** * Life sciences research experience/expertise **REQUIRED** * Good understanding of pharmacology (e.g., pharmacodynamics, routes of administration, receptor biology, signaling pathways) **REQUIRED** * 1\-2 years of experience in the pharmaceutical industry, life sciences research experience, and/or life sciences consulting **PREFERRED** * Attention to detail – Ability to meticulously review deliverables before submitting them for final QC (can you spot errors in your own work?) * Proactivity – at FENIX, we don't simply follow instructions; instead, we critically evaluate whether the instructions are complete or could be applied beyond their initial scope; in addition, providing new ideas on process improvements/efficiencies and methodologies is expected * Resourcefulness – the ability to independently search for an answer and ask for help when needed * Proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel); the ability to craft visual and professional PowerPoint slides from scratch is a big plus * Demonstrated ability to work independently (good work ethic, maturity, and personal initiative), as well as ability to work as a member of a highly collaborative team * ‪Self\-discipline for planning and organizing tasks, ensuring that no task (no matter how minor) is forgotten * The ability to aggregate, organize, distill, and analyze relevant information to derive meaningful implications * Flexibility and ability to adapt to changing project and client needs (while you may initially begin on one project, you may have to do other projects based on changing company needs) * Willingness and ability to travel to domestic and international scientific congresses * Growth mindset, with the ability to work collaboratively as well as autonomously * Ability to think critically and strategically and make assessments keeping the "big picture" in mind, frequently asking yourself: "Does this make sense?" * Humility and ability to take feedback constructively, with a strong desire to learn and grow * The ability to take complex information and make it easy to understand to sometimes non\-scientific audiences

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Requisition ID: 73816 Date: Mar 31, 2026 Location: Dublin, L, IE Department: Operations Description: At West, we’re a dedicated team that is connected by a purpose to improve patient lives that has been at the center of our Company for more than a century. Our story began when Herman O. West solved the problem of supplying penicillin in mass quantities to the US Government during World War 2\. Through our work to deliver thousands of life\-saving and life\-enhancing injectable medicines to millions of patients daily, West’s indelible mark on the healthcare industry has just begun. A name started our story. How will yours help write our future? There’s no better place to join an inclusive community of professionals with opportunities for lifelong learning, growth and development. Supported by benefit programs, we empower the physical, mental, emotional and financial health of our team members and their families. We believe in giving back to help those in need in the communities where we live and work. And are equally committed to creating a healthier environment and planet through our sustainability efforts. #### **Job Summary** Manages the maintenance and repair of equipment in operation at West. Liaises with external contractors for the maintenance and repair of same. The Maintenance Electrician is also responsible for using the preventative maintenance system to pre\-determined guidelines. Reporting directly to the Maintenance Lead Technician. Become a mentor to apprentice electricians and support their development.**Essential Duties and Responsibilities** ----------------------------------------- * Responsible for the maintenance and breakdown repair of moulding equipment (including among others, moulding machines, automation/robots, water heaters, oil heaters, conveyors, and hot runner controllers). * Work with the Production Managers to troubleshoot equipment/plant problems. * Establishes relationships with the customers and suppliers in pursuit of continuous improvement and on\-going business development. * Attends, schedules, and facilitates internal meetings to help establish priorities and assign tasks * Solves, in conjunction with the Quality department, customer related problems/issues as needed * In conjunction with approved vendors, research new processes and/or systems for maintenance/manufacture of equipment and implement new processes and systems as developed under the direction of the Operations Manager. * Instructs others, formally or informally, regarding manufacturing related skills or knowledge and assist in the training of employees on new processes or systems introduced to the company * Compliance to all site Environmental, Health and Safety requirements, training, and regulations * Support and contribute to Lean Sigma programs and activities towards delivery of the set target * Perform additional duties at the request of the direct manager **Education** ------------- * Associate's Degree Time served QQI Level 6 Advanced Certificate Craft Electrical required * Bachelor's Degree Engineering preferred **Work Experience** ------------------- * Minimum 3 years experience is required in troubleshooting various manufacturing equipment. **Preferred Knowledge, Skills and Abilities** --------------------------------------------- * Sound knowledge of CMMS and MS Office * Any other certifications are a plus. * Support and contribute to Lean Sigma programs and activities towards delivery of the set target. * Able to always comply with the company’s safety policy. * Able to always comply with the company’s quality policy. * Knowledge of PLC Programming and electrical work * Ability to read electrical, mechanical, and hydraulic/pneumatic drawings is essential * Ability to interact with suppliers, customers, and external contractors **Travel Requirements** ----------------------- 5%: Up to 13 business days per year**Physical Requirements** ------------------------- Medium\-Exerting up to 50lb/22kg of force occasionally and/or up to 20lbs/9kg of force frequently, and/or up to 10lbs/4kgs of force constantly to move objects.**Additional Requirements** --------------------------- * Must be able to lift 35 lbs. continuously and up to 75 lbs. occasionally, with or without assistance. **Shift** --------- 2 cycle shift pattern (shifts are 8am \- 8pm \& include every 2nd weekend) \#LI\-MMM \#LI\-Onsite West is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. If you have a special need that requires accommodation in order to apply to West, please send an email to Apply.Accommodation@westpharma.com. Where permitted by law, an offer of employment with West Pharmaceutical Services, or any of its subsidiary or affiliate companies, is contingent upon the satisfactory completion of background screening.

title: Manufacturing Process Technician
description: **Job Description Summary** The Manufacturing Process Technician at Amgen plays a crucial role in the operation and maintenance of manufacturing equipment. This individual is responsible for monitoring and adjusting manufacturing processes, troubleshooting any issues, and ensuring the quality of final products. They work in compliance with company policies and safety regulations. **Hiring Requirements:** * Manufacturing Process, Packing and Inspection Technicians are required to work a variety of shift patterns including days, 2, 3 and 4 cycle shifts. * They will perform all operations with due care and attention and in accordance with Good Manufacturing Practices and Amgen requirements. * They will be involved in problem solving and troubleshooting including initiation and documentation of investigations. * They will be responsible for the performance of self\-inspection Quality \& Safety Audits within their functional area. * They will be part of a learning and development program which will include annual reviews and goal setting via the Maximizing Amgen Performance (MAP) process. They will be responsible for the cross training of other colleagues. * They will be responsible for the use, review, revision and upgrade of operational documentation and peer review of operations. * They will play a key role in the development of the manufacturing systems and will be responsible for continuous improvement initiatives. Proactively identifying operational improvement opportunities or process related issues including escalation \& follow up with area Sr. Associate Tech Engineers for effective resolution and implementation. * They will be champions for safe working practices and safety initiatives within their functional area. * They will carry out any sampling, testing and inspections that may be required. They will also carry out calibration and maintenance of test equipment. * Contribute and assist with Corporate, FDA, IMB Auditors and other regulatory bodies during company audits. * They will carry out any other duty as may be designated by supervisor/ management from time to time. **Qualifications:** * Educated to Leaving Certificate standard or equivalent (minimum five passes including English and Mathematics required). * Applicants should have some relevant experience of operation of a manufacturing function in a sterile pharmaceutical manufacturing or similar environment. * Be a results\-oriented person with excellent organization, communication and team development skills with an ability to successfully interact with people, create a team environment and generate co\-operation. * Demonstrated ability to deliver to team, site and personal objectives. * Demonstrated understanding and use of RFT techniques and lean manufacturing concepts. **Preferred Skills:** Experience working with equipment utilised in the manufacture of parenteral products such as autoclaves, filling lines, formulation vessels plus: * Good mechanical aptitude * Excellent problem\-solving skills * Ability to work in a team environment and on one’s own initiative

title: Manufacturing Process Technician- Inspection
description: **MPT\- Inspection** **Job Description** The Manufacturing Process Technician at Amgen plays a crucial role in the operation and maintenance of manufacturing equipment. This individual is responsible for monitoring and adjusting manufacturing processes, troubleshooting any issues, and ensuring the quality of final products. They work in compliance with company policies and safety regulations. **Job Description Summary** The Manufacturing Process Technician reports to the Shift Manager and will be responsible for one or more of a number of roles relating to the manufacture and Inspection of sterile parenteral drugs to include but not limited to operation and set up of manufacturing equipment, processing of parts and components for manufacturing, performing transactions in electronic systems such as EBR, LIMS, SAP etc. and cleaning and sanitization of production areas. This job specification outlines the general responsibilities associated with the role of Manufacturing Process Technician on the Amgen Manufacturing Site. The site is focused on continuous improvement of all work processes and practices and all colleagues are required to be flexible in this regard. This means that they may be asked to carry out additional work functions that are not described in this specification but are associated with their role. *Please note successful candidates will be required to work a shift pattern.* **Hiring Requirements:** * Manufacturing Process, Packing and Inspection Technicians are required to work a variety of shift patterns including days, 2, 3 and 4 cycle shifts. * They will perform all operations with due care and attention and in accordance with Good Manufacturing Practices and Amgen requirements. * They will be involved in problem solving and troubleshooting including initiation and documentation of investigations. * They will be responsible for the performance of self\-inspection Quality \& Safety Audits within their functional area. * They will be part of a learning and development program which will include annual reviews and goal setting via the Maximizing Amgen Performance (MAP) process. They will be responsible for the cross training of other colleagues. * They will be responsible for the use, review, revision and upgrade of operational documentation and peer review of operations. * They will play a key role in the development of the manufacturing systems and will be responsible for continuous improvement initiatives. Proactively identifying operational improvement opportunities or process related issues including escalation \& follow up with area Sr. Associate Tech Engineers for effective resolution and implementation. * They will be champions for safe working practices and safety initiatives within their functional area. * They will carry out any sampling, testing and inspections that may be required. They will also carry out calibration and maintenance of test equipment. * Contribute and assist with Corporate, FDA, HPRA Auditors and other regulatory bodies during company audits. * They will carry out any other duty as may be designated by supervisor/ management from time to time. **Qualifications:** * Educated to Leaving Certificate standard or equivalent (minimum five passes including English and Mathematics required). * A third level qualification in a relevant discipline would be an advantage. * Applicants should have some relevant experience of operation of a manufacturing function in a sterile pharmaceutical manufacturing or similar environment. * Be a results\-oriented person with excellent organization, communication and team development skills with an ability to successfully interact with people, create a team environment and generate co\-operation. * Demonstrated ability to deliver to team, site and personal objectives. * Demonstrated understanding and use of RFT techniques and lean manufacturing concepts. **Preferred Skills:** Experience working with equipment utilised in the manufacture of parenteral products such as Automated visual Inspection, filling lines, formulation vessels plus: * Good mechanical aptitude * Excellent problem\-solving skills * Ability to work in a team environment and on one’s own initiative
